findings.title: Any trip member (incl. Relaxer) can fabricate settlements between two OTHER members, corrupting everyone's shared balance graph, and cannot undo their own
findings.rootCause: create_settlement() deliberately does not require the caller to be one of the two parties ('the caller does NOT have to be one of the two parties', settlements.py:144-152). The only gate is `_is_accepted_member(cursor, trip_id, user_id)` (line 233), which returns True for every role including Relaxer. The amount cap (total_spend*1.01, line 305) bounds the size but not the act of fabrication. The delete authorization (line 521: `user_id != owner_id and user_id != row['from_user_id']`) excludes the recorder, so a third-party who records cannot retract it.

findings.title: POST /api/settlements does not re-verify the two parties are STILL accepted members at read time, and snapshots names only — a kicked member's fabricated settlement persists in the balance graph
findings.rootCause: Settlements are immutable post-creation and /api/data (data.py:1178-1184) selects ALL settlements for the trip with no re-check that from_user_id/to_user_id are still members. remove_trip_member (trips.py:1103) deletes the trip_members row but does not sweep or flag the member's settlements.

findings.title: /api/sync expense and trip_days loops KeyError → uncaught 500 on a bulk payload entry missing `id` or `tripId`
findings.rootCause: The per-row loops use subscript access (`e['id']`, `e['tripId']`, `d['id']`, `d['tripId']`) instead of `.get()` with a skip/continue, unlike the per-row delta endpoints which validate id presence and return a clean 400. The SEC-2 hardening covered non-dict BODIES but not missing per-row keys inside the bulk arrays.

findings.title: DELETE /api/expenses/<id> is the only mutating route with no per-route rate limit (relies solely on the 200/min global default)
findings.rootCause: Missing @limiter.limit decorator on delete_expense; the global default_limits=['200 per minute'] (extensions.py:25) silently covers it but at a much higher rate than intended for a write path.

findings.title: upsert_expense uses `request.json or {}` instead of the SEC-2 json_body() helper, so a POST without application/json content-type 415s/400s before the isinstance guard
findings.rootCause: The SEC-2 sweep (json_body across write routes) missed expenses.py — it kept the legacy `request.json or {}` idiom that the helper was introduced to replace.

findings.title: /components design-system gallery is served publicly with no auth in production
findings.rootCause: components_preview() has no @require_auth and no dev-env gate (unlike the JWT-secret and rate-limit guards which check _is_dev_env()). It renders templates/components.html unconditionally.
